patient.query: I guess it happens a lot in social situations, like when I’m with friends or even at work. I keep worrying about saying the wrong thing or making a fool of myself. Sometimes, it feels like everyone knows what I’m thinking, and it just makes me want to shut down. It’s hard to even enjoy being around people when my mind is racing like that.

automatic_thoughts: worrying about saying the wrong thing

patient.query: Yeah, it’s just that feeling of being so exposed, you know? It’s like I’m on display, and I can’t help but think everyone is judging me. I want to be more relaxed and just be myself, but that voice in my head makes it hard. It’s frustrating because I know I can be fun and lighthearted, but it feels so far away sometimes.

neutral: Fear of being in a small group (finding)
Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)
Fear of being a burden to others (finding)

patient.query: I mean, I can’t really point to anything specific... It’s just a gut feeling, I guess. Like, when I’m talking, I might catch someone’s expression, and it makes me think they’re not interested or that I’m not making sense. It’s like this spiral starts where I start doubting myself and thinking everyone is judging me in the moment. Even when people don’t say anything, it just feels like... they see right through me.

entailment: Ability to understand own emotions (observable entity)
neutral: Fear of speaking to people in authority (finding)

patient.query: Yeah, I suppose there are certain situations that make it worse. Like, big gatherings or even meetings where I have to speak up. My heart starts racing, and the thoughts just flood in. But it’s also weird, because sometimes I can be okay and enjoy myself, and then out of nowhere, it just switches. It’s like there's this switch that flips, and I can’t figure out why.

triggers: meetings where I have to speak up

patient.query: I wish I could pin it down better. Sometimes it feels like it can just be a look from someone or even the silence that follows when I say something. And then, my mind starts racing with thoughts like “What did I say wrong?” or “Do they think I’m silly?” It all gets jumbled up, and it’s hard to catch my breath. It’s like my brain just goes on overdrive.

triggers: silence that follows when I say something
